question: US forces enter Iran by April 30?
narrative_context: White House official March 26, 2026: Trump 'has no current plans to send troops' to Iran (official statement)
VP Vance told Netanyahu he's overselling regime change—admission of limited US leverage (WaPo, April 1 2026)
Ground campaign would require massive resources and Congressional approval unlikely in current political climate (CSIS, April 2026)
Active air campaign ongoing, but ground invasion is a different escalation level with zero stated planning
thesis: Market likely prices troops at ~25-30%. Reality: ~5%. White House explicitly denies plans. VP Vance's remarks about limited leverage suggest the US has no appetite for ground ops. Ground invasion requires Congressional approval, massive resource commitment, and sustained political will. None of these exist. This is a near-certain NO.

White House (March 26): Trump 'has no current plans to send troops.' VP Vance just told Netanyahu he's overselling regime change.
Ground invasion requires Congressional approval, massive resources, sustained political will. None present.
Markets think 25% odds. Reality: <5%. Official statements + political reality = clear NO. https://dub.sh/pb-x
telegram_post:
🚁 **US Forces Enter Iran by April 30? Market Mispriced by 20pp+**

**The setup:**
Polymarket shows 25.5pp gap on US ground forces entering Iran by April 30.

**Why <5% is right:**
• White House official (March 26): Trump "has no current plans to send troops"
• VP Vance told Netanyahu he's overselling regime change—admission of limited US leverage
• Ground invasion needs Congressional approval (unlikely in current climate)
• Air campaign ≠ ground troops. These are fundamentally different escalation levels
• ZERO stated planning for ground operations

Bet the fundamentals.
